verbGlossary.SCOPED UNDER: {source}'s scope of work is defined by {target} division or section (e.g., electrician scoped under MasterFormat Division 26 Electrical)
verbGlossary.BILLED AGAINST: {source}cost for the resource are charged to a line item in {target} classification (e.g., plumber hours billed against standard cost code for plumbing rough-in)
verbGlossary.ASSIGNED TO: {source} is allocated to a specific element in {target} breakdown structure (e.g., crane assigned to a WBS code for structural steel erection)
verbGlossary.SPECIFIED BY: {source} must meet the performance and quality requirements defined in {target} (e.g., concrete mix design specified by Division 03 specification section)
verbGlossary.ESTIMATED UNDER: {source} quantity and cost are budgeted within {target} code's allocation (e.g., rebar tonnage estimated under the concrete budget code line)
verbGlossary.INSPECTS PER: {source} or quality professional conducts field inspections following {target} hazard categories or protocols (e.g., safety professional inspects per OSHA Focus Four hazard categories)
verbGlossary.INSTALLS PER: {source} performs installation in compliance with {target} prescriptive requirements (e.g., sprinkler fitter installs per NFPA 13 spacing and coverage rules)
verbGlossary.TESTED PER: {source} or system is tested following {target} prescribed protocol and acceptance criteria (e.g., fire protection materials tested per NFPA fire resistance ratings)
verbGlossary.REPORTS PER: {source} documents safety events using {target} recordkeeping format and timelines (e.g., safety professional logs incidents per OSHA 300 log requirements)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES PER: Inspector assigns injury or deficiency codes using {target} classification taxonomy (e.g., QC inspector classifies injury type per ANSI Z16 body part and nature codes)
verbGlossary.PERMITTED UNDER: {source} installation requires a permit or inspection governed by {target} code (e.g., elevator installation permitted under IBC Chapter 30 elevator provisions)
verbGlossary.RATED UNDER: {source}'s thermal, energy, or fire performance is rated according to {target} thresholds (e.g., insulation R-value rated under energy code climate zone requirements)
verbGlossary.VERIFIES FOR: {source} agent confirms system performance meets the target certification's credit requirements (e.g., CxA verifies mechanical systems for LEED Enhanced Commissioning credit)
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIED UNDER: {source} or element is categorized within {target} hierarchical taxonomy (e.g., glass assemblies classified under UniFormat B2020 Exterior Windows)
verbGlossary.BENCHMARKED TO: {source} productivity or cost rate is compared against {target} published industry norms (e.g., surveyor layout rates benchmarked to AACE standard labor productivity factors)
verbGlossary.MOBILIZED PER: {source} is deployed to site based on the target delivery method's phasing and sequencing requirements (e.g., earthmoving equipment mobilized per design-build fast-track phasing plan)
verbGlossary.MAPPED TO: {source} is cross-referenced to {target} mapping structure for consistent reporting (e.g., electrical wire purchases mapped to MasterFormat Division 26 for cost roll-up)
